2606.06087.title: LatentSkill: From In-Context Textual Skills to In-Weight Latent Skills for LLM Agents
2606.06087.paper_summary: Agent systems increasingly use textual skills to encode reusable task procedures, but injecting these skills into the prompt at every step incurs substantial context overhead and exposes skill content as plaintext. We present LatentSkill, a framework that converts textual skills into plug-and-play LoRA adapters through a pretrained hypernetwork. LatentSkill stores skill knowledge in weight space rather than context space, removing per-step skill tokens while preserving modular loading, scaling, and composition. On ALFWorld and Search-QA, LatentSkill outperforms the corresponding in-context skill baseline while using substantially fewer prefill tokens: it improves ALFWorld success by 21.4 and 13.4 points on the seen and unseen splits with 64.1% fewer prefill tokens, and improves Search-QA exact match by 3.0 points with 72.2% lower skill-token overhead. Further analysis shows that generated skill LoRAs form a structured semantic geometry, can be precisely controlled via the LoRA scaling coefficient, and can be composed through parameter-space arithmetic when skill components are aligned. These findings suggest that weight-space skills provide an efficient, modular, and less exposed substrate for extending LLM agents.
2606.06087.paper_summary_zh: 代理系統越來越多地使用文本技能來編碼可重用的任務程序，但在每一步都將這些技能注入提示中會產生可觀的上下文開銷，並將技能內容以明文形式暴露。我們提出了LatentSkill，一個通過預訓練的超網絡將文本技能轉換為即插即用的LoRA適配器的框架。LatentSkill將技能知識存儲在權重空間中，而不是上下文空間中，去除了每步的技能標記，同時保留了模塊化加載、縮放和組合的能力。在ALFWorld和Search-QA上，LatentSkill的表現超過了相應的上下文技能基準，同時使用了顯著更少的預填標記：在已見和未見的分割中，ALFWorld的成功率分別提高了21.4和13.4點，並減少了64.1%的預填標記，而Search-QA的精確匹配提高了3.0點，技能標記開銷降低了72.2%。進一步的分析顯示，生成的技能LoRA形成了一種結構化的語義幾何，可以通過LoRA縮放係數進行精確控制，並且當技能組件對齊時，可以通過參數空間算術進行組合。這些發現表明，權重空間技能提供了一種高效、模塊化且不易暴露的基底，用於擴展LLM代理。
2606.06087.author: Aofan Yu et.al.
2606.06087.authors: Aofan Yu, Chenyu Zhou, Tianyi Xu, Zihan Guo, Rong Shan, Zhihui Fu, Jun Wang, Weiwen Liu, Yong Yu, Weinan Zhang, Jianghao Lin
